title: Jack Dongarra's Contributions to High-Performance Computing in the Cloud Era
description: A visual breakdown of Jack Dongarra's key contributions and how they relate to and enable modern cloud computing infrastructure.
elements: Cloud Computing Platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP)
relationships: Dongarra's LINPACK/LAPACK provides foundational linear algebra routines used in scientific computing, a key workload on HPC cloud instances.
The TOP500 list, co-founded by Dongarra, uses the LINPACK benchmark (HPL) to rank systems, many of which are now accessed via cloud models.
MPI, which Dongarra helped standardize, is essential for parallel computing across distributed systems, the architecture that underpins both supercomputers and cloud data centers.
Cloud providers offer HPC services that rely on the software ecosystems and performance metrics pioneered by Dongarra's work.
